name: Texas Barbecued Beef Brisket
recipeInstructions:
1. Thoroughly rub the brisket on all sides with the barbecue rub and allow it to come to room temperature.
2. In a covered grill, build a small fire on one side as far up against one wall as possible. Place the brisket on the opposite side from the fire so that none of the brisket is directly over the flame. Keep the top closed, sit down, and grab the cooler. This is where you learn about the Zen of Barbecue.
- Maintain a temperature between 180 and 220°F using a thermometer on your covered grill.
- Develop your own personal refueling policy; for example, use one handful of coals or wood chunks per beer.
3. Cook for about 8 to 10 hours or as long as you can manage. The brisket will darken on the outside, which is a personal favorite.
4. Once done, remove the brisket, trim excess fat, and slice it thin. Serve with barbecue sauce on the side—no professional would cover properly cooked brisket with sauce; they'd just dab a touch.
The key to this recipe is patience and enjoying a day of relaxation. Pair with plenty of tall drinks.
